title: AN ACT SEPARATING THE KAMORA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL - TAWANGAN ANNEX IN BARANGAYS TAWANGAN AND LUSOD, MUNICIPALITY OF KABAYAN, PROVINCE OF BENGUET FROM THE KAMORA NATIONAL HTGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS TAWANGAN-LUSOD NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
H. No. 5404; 106 OG No. 11, 1400 (March 15 2010)

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 9901, December 31, 2009 ]

AN ACT SEPARATING THE KAMORA NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL - TAWANGAN ANNEX IN BARANGAYS TAWANGAN AND LUSOD, MUNICIPALITY OF KABAYAN, PROVINCE OF BENGUET FROM THE KAMORA NATIONAL HTGH SCHOOL, CONVERTING IT INTO AN INDEPENDENT NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS TAWANGAN-LUSOD NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1.

Separation and Conversion into a National High School.

- The Kamora National High School â Tawangan Annex in barangays Tawangan and Lusod, Municipality of Kabayan, Province of Benguet is hereby separated from the Kamora National High School and converted into an independent national high school to be known as Tawangan-Lusod National High School.

SEC. 2.

Transfer of Assets and Liabilities.

- All personnel, assets, liabilities and records of the Kamora National High School - Tawangan Annex are hereby transferred to and absorbed by the Tawangan-Lusod National High School.

SEC. 3.

Appropriations.

- The Secretary of Education shall immediately include in the Department's program the operationalization of the Tawangan-Lusod National High School, the initial funding of which shall be charged against the current year's appropriations of the Kamora National High School -Tawangan Annex. Thereafter, the amount necessary for the continued operation of the school shall be included in the annual General Appropriations Act.

SEC. 4.

Implementation.

- The Secretary of Education shall issue rules and regulations that may be necessary to carry out the purpose of this Act.

SEC. 4.

Implementation.

- The Secretary of Education shall issue rules and regulations that may be necessary to carry out the purpose of this Act.

SEC. 5.

Effectivity.

- This Act shall take effect fifteen (15) days after its publication in the

Official Gazette.

Approved,

(Sgd.) JUAN PONCE ENRILE

(Sgd.) PROSPERO C. NOGRALES

President of Senate

Speaker of the House of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on December 3,2008 and September 30,2009, respectively.

(Sgd.) EMMA LIRIO-REYES

(Sgd.) MARILYN B. BARUA-YAP

Secretary of Senate

Secretary General House of Representatives

Approved: DEC 31 2009

(Sgd.) GLORIA MACAPAGAL - ARROYO

President of the Philippines
